plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, as per conservative mandate.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for stable income and broad market exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
This plan aims for capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio risk.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets a modest ~1.5%/month, prioritizing capital preservation. Expect lower volatility, but also limited upside potential. Risk of significant drawdown is reduced, but not eliminated.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall leverage.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce QQQI to 15% to balance income with growth opportunities.
Buy CRM at 10% and META at 10% to add high-scoring quality tech names.
This plan balances risk and growth, maintaining a moderate cash buffer.
Keep total adds within limits of trims and cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for ~2.5%/month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration, blending broad index with quality tech. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns.

moves.note: Add leveraged ETF with high score

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits, allowing for other high-conviction adds.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit losing leveraged ETF positions.
Buy CRM at 15% and ZS at 15% to concentrate in high-scoring tech setups.
Add TECS at 5% to reintroduce a leveraged ETF with a strong scanner read.
This plan uses leverage and concentration for growth, with strict stops.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer, focusing on pressing what is working.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretch growth. It uses leverage and concentrates in high-scoring setups, which significantly raises both upside and drawdown potential. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, and this mode can lose a lot fast; strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell all SOXL, SPAL, CRWD, and SMH to eliminate leveraged ETFs and high-beta names.
Increase QQQI to 40% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to shift focus from growth to income.
This plan aims to park cash for yield, prioritizing low drawdown.
Maintain a cash buffer, understanding distributions are variable.
income.read: This income approach targets ~1%/month, focusing on yield and low drawdown. It rotates into income/covered-call ETFs and broad index, cutting leverage and high-beta names. Distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff; this is cash-like income, not a savings account.
